proposed_description: The free-choice indefinites take the subjunctive in a subordinate clause: Chiunque sia, digli che non ci sono; Qualunque cosa faccia, la fa bene; Dovunque tu vada, ti seguiro. Structurally distinct from the sibling leaves: the trigger is neither a matrix verb (opinion/emotion) nor a conjunction (sebbene, prima che) nor a polarity flip, but an indefinite sitting in the subordinate's own subject or object slot. Almost no colloquial slippage, like the other structural triggers.

canonical_examples: Chiunque sia, digli che non ci sono
Qualunque cosa faccia, la fa bene
proposed_attributes.common_miss: indicative after a free-choice word (qualunque cosa fa, chiunque e)

rationale: NOT for me to author -- proposed and left uncited, per stay-in-role. The parent has exactly four children (opinion / emotion / hypothetical conjunctions / negation) and none reaches the free-choice indefinites; grep confirms ZERO items in grammar_questions_tense_choice.json use chiunque, qualunque, qualsiasi or dovunque. Meanwhile BOTH my free-choice leaves declare tense_choice.indicative_vs_subjunctive as a prerequisite, so the shipped tree has a prerequisite pointing at a parent whose children do not cover it. OIC states the rule outright and it is currently taught by no item in the estate. Full argument and the who-authors-it question: inter_chat/Architecture_IndefiniteAuthor_free_choice_mood_seam.md.

proposed_description: The collective stand-alone forms: Ho visto tutto (everything), Tutti sono venuti (everybody, plural verb), Va tutto bene. Distinct from indefinite.core.tutto, which is tutto + article in front of a noun, and distinct from ognuno, which distributes one-by-one over a group and takes a SINGULAR verb. The tutti/ognuno contrast is the diagnostic: tutti hanno portato qualcosa vs ognuno ha portato qualcosa.

proposed_attributes.common_miss: singular verb with tutti (by analogy with ognuno); tutti i as a pronoun (tutti i sono venuti).

rationale: The pronoun aggregate's OWN description promises the set is 'Somebody, something, nobody, nothing, everybody' -- and no leaf delivers everybody as a collective. Ognuno covers 'each one' distributively, which is a different machine and takes the opposite verb agreement. So Tutti sono venuti and Ho visto tutto currently have nowhere to be marked. Found while authoring ind_oc_02, where 'Tutti' is a plausible learner answer I had to guard against with no bucket to attribute it to. Cited on no item pending registration.

proposed_description: The free-choice place word: Dovunque tu vada, ti seguiro; Ovunque si guardi, c'e traffico. Invariable, adverbial, and it takes the subjunctive in a subordinate exactly as chiunque and qualunque do. Dovunque and ovunque are interchangeable.

proposed_attributes.common_miss: indicative in the subordinate (dovunque vai); dovunque used for a bare 'everywhere' (dappertutto).

rationale: OIC names dovunque in the same rule as qualunque/qualsiasi/chiunque, and it has no home anywhere in the estate. It is an ADVERB, so it is neither my qualunque_qualsiasi (adjectival) nor my chiunque (pronoun); and the adverb tree carries only a generic position leaf, per the boundary note Architecture added to the adverb dispatch. Proposed under my free_choice aggregate because the free-choice semantics and the subjunctive behaviour are what define it, but I have no strong claim -- if it belongs to adverb, say so and I will stay off it. Cited on no item.
